subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze shapes with both Line and Rotational Symmetry
Apply symmetry principles to Tessellations and Tilings
Predict symmetry changes after shape transformations
Design complex symmetrical patterns (Mandala or Rangoli logic)

lesson_plan.title: Combining Line and Rotational Symmetry
topics_covered:
Regular polygons: The perfect 'Common Ground' 

[Image of lines of symmetry in regular polygons]
Shapes with rotation but NO line symmetry (e.g., Parallelogram, 'Z')
Shapes with line but NO rotation symmetry (e.g., Isosceles triangle, 'A')
student_activities: Sort a set of 20 shapes into a Venn Diagram
Draw a shape that has Order 3 rotation but zero lines of symmetry

lesson_plan.title: Symmetry in Tilings and Nature
topics_covered: Using symmetry to simplify area/perimeter of complex figures
student_activities: Create a small tessellation using a regular hexagon
Identify the 'Unit' that is being reflected/rotated in a complex tile pattern
assessment: Project: Create a 'Symmetry Portfolio' with nature/art examples

teaching_aids: High-res images of snowflakes and crystals
teacher_tip: Encourage students to look at a parallelogram—it feels symmetrical, but try to fold it! It’s the perfect lesson on why rotation and reflection are different 'grounds'.
